1.texto_original: L’orazione del profeta Abacuc, sopra Sighionot.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Abacuc 3:1

"L’orazione del profeta Abacuc, sopra Sighionot."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Abacuc 3:1

Philological Analysis

"L’orazione del profeta Abacuc, sopra Sighionot."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"L’orazione del profeta Abacuc, sopra Sighionot."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

15.texto_original: Tu camminasti co’ tuoi cavalli sopra il mare, Sopra il mucchio delle grandi acque.
15.normal:
Devotional Study of Abacuc 3:15

"Tu camminasti co’ tuoi cavalli sopra il mare, Sopra il mucchio delle grandi acque."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
15.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Abacuc 3:15

Philological Analysis

"Tu camminasti co’ tuoi cavalli sopra il mare, Sopra il mucchio delle grandi acque."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
15.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Tu camminasti co’ tuoi cavalli sopra il mare, Sopra il mucchio delle grandi acque."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

16.texto_original: Or io ho udito, e le mie viscere si sono commosse, Le mie labbra han tremato a quella voce, Un tarlo mi è entrato nelle ossa, Io son tutto spaventato in me stesso; Come avrei io riposo nel giorno della distretta, Quando colui che darà il guasto al popolo salirà contro a lui?

17.texto_original: Perciocchè il fico non germoglierà, E non vi sarà frutto alcuno nelle viti; La rendita dell’ulivo fallirà, E i campi non produrranno cibo; Le gregge verranno meno nelle mandre, E non vi saranno più buoi nelle stalle.
17.normal:
Devotional Study of Abacuc 3:17

"Perciocchè il fico non germoglierà, E non vi sarà frutto alcuno nelle viti; La rendita dell’ulivo fallirà, E i campi non produrranno cibo; Le gregge verranno meno nelle mandre, E non vi saranno più buoi nelle stalle."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

19.texto_original: Il Signore Iddio mio è la mia forza, E renderà i miei piedi simili a que’ delle cerve, E mi condurrà sopra i miei alti luoghi. Dato al Capo de’ Musici, sopra Neghinot.
19.normal:
Devotional Study of Abacuc 3:19

"Il Signore Iddio mio è la mia forza, E renderà i miei piedi simili a que’ delle cerve, E mi condurrà sopra i miei alti luoghi. Dato al Capo de’ Musici, sopra Neghinot."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.
